name: django
summary: A high-level Python web framework that encourages rapid development and clean, pragmatic design

story.problem: Building a web application involves solving the same problems every time: connecting to a database, managing user authentication, handling form validation, generating HTML, and building an admin interface. Django was designed so you'd never have to solve those problems again — it comes with everything pre-integrated.
story.mental_model: Django follows the MTV pattern (Model, Template, View). Models are Python classes that map to database tables via a built-in ORM. Views are functions or classes that receive a request and return a response. Templates are HTML files with a simple tag syntax. Django wires them together with a URL dispatcher. The 'opinionated' structure means every Django project looks the same.
story.when_to_use: Use Django for full-stack web applications that need user accounts, an admin interface for content management, forms with validation, or an ORM for complex data relationships. It's the right choice when you want a complete, production-ready framework with a well-trodden path for common features.
story.when_not_to_use: Django is heavy for pure REST APIs — the ORM, templates, and admin panel add weight you don't use. FastAPI is a better starting point for API-only projects. Django's synchronous-by-default nature (though async support has improved) can also be a constraint for highly concurrent applications.

alternatives.name: fastapi
alternatives.reason: Purpose-built for async REST APIs with automatic validation and docs — much lighter than Django REST Framework

alternatives.name: flask
alternatives.reason: Microframework — start here if you want to assemble your own stack piece by piece

alternatives.name: wagtail
alternatives.reason: CMS built on Django — use it when you need Django's power with a polished content editing interface

nodes.label: django
nodes.summary: Top-level module. Import as `import django` or `from django import ...`
